summary: This indicator tracks a small number of incidents each year, so it can swing sharply in percentage terms. DOC's own reporting ties the FY22-to-FY24 decline to broader efforts to better manage the jails, and the FY25 rebound to a nearly 10 percent rise in average daily population, a larger share of people held on serious violent charges following bail reform, uniformed staff attrition, and a state prison workers' wildcat strike that temporarily halted transfers of sentenced individuals out of city custody.
oneLiner: decreasing overall, though FY25 saw a rebound tied to jail population growth
